issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` and only provides `outline-offset: 3px` and `border-radius`, which do not create a visible focus ring. This removes the default browser focus indicator without providing a visible replacement, making keyboard navigation impossible to track for sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` selector sets `outline: none` without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border in the same rule block. Combined with the broken global `:focus-visible` rule, this leaves buttons without any focus indicator, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `overflow: visible`, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it in the accessibility tree. This renders the utility non-functional, potentially exposing screen-reader text visually or hiding it incorrectly.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn

issuesFound.fix: Line 821: #site-search button[type="submit"] { ... width: 44px; height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the checkbox difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1440: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but falls short of the 44px recommended target size, potentially making links difficult to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1476: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { ... min-height: 44px; ... }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: This button includes transform in its transition, creating movement effects without a reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 641: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses transition: transform, which moves the element when it appears. Without a reduced-motion override, this animation cannot be disabled by users who need reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 641: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards transition the transform property on hover, creating a lifting motion. No reduced-motion media query disables this effect, violating user motion preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 641: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph text in section headers uses #aaaaaa (var(--colour-text-secondary)) on white background, yielding approximately 2.3:1 contrast. This is below the 4.5:1 threshold required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 756: .section-header p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .logo-bar-label
issuesFound.explanation: The label text uses var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on white background, resulting in approximately 2.5:1 contrast ratio. This fails WCAG AA requirements for text visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1066: .logo-bar-label { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card paragraphs use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, providing only 2.3:1 contrast. This is insufficient for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1132: .feature-card p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .products-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The section header paragraph uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white, yielding 2.3:1 contrast. This does not meet WCAG AA standards for text contrast.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1236: .products-section .section-header p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .pricing-section .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing section header text uses #aaaaaa on white background, providing approximately 2.3:1 contrast. This is below the required 4.5:1 threshold.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1284: .pricing-section .section-header p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .contact-section > p
issuesFound.explanation: The contact section description uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, resulting in 2.3:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1428: .contact-section > p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints use #aaaaaa text on white background, providing only 2.3:1 contrast. This is insufficient for users with low vision to read helper text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1456: .input-hint { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-brand address
issuesFound.explanation: The address text uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on var(--colour-neutral-900) (#0f172a) background, resulting in approximately 3.3:1 contrast. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1470: .footer-brand address { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-400); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom text uses #64748b on dark background (#0f172a), yielding approximately 3.3:1 contrast. This is below the WCAG AA threshold of 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1482: .footer-bottom > p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-400); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .price-period
issuesFound.explanation: The price period text (e.g., "/month") uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, resulting in 2.3:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA standards.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: .price-period { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .product-body > p:not(.product-tagline)
issuesFound.explanation: Product descriptions use #aaaaaa on white background, providing only 2.3:1 contrast. This is insufficient for reading product details.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1384: .product-body > p:not(.product-tagline) { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .plan-tagline
issuesFound.explanation: Plan taglines use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, resulting in 2.3:1 contrast. This fails the minimum contrast requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1308: .plan-tagline { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .testimonial-card figcaption p
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonial author role text uses #aaaaaa on white background, yielding 2.3:1 contrast. This is below the required 4.5:1 ratio.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1402: .testimonial-card figcaption p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-700); ... }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator. Buttons are interactive elements that must show clear focus states for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 417: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit dimensions of width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 44×44px minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). Users with motor impairments or on touch devices may struggle to activate this control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 569: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto instead of the correct screen-reader-only technique. This means content intended to be visually hidden but announced to screen readers will be fully visible and not properly hidden from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 353: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element has transform: translateY(-200%) and transition: transform var(--transition-base) for its reveal animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 453 is empty and does not override this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 453: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary variable is set to #aaaaaa and is used on --colour-bg-page which resolves to #ffffff. This produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3). Users with low vision or colour blindness may not read this text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 52: --colour-text-secondary: #64748b;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on headings
issuesFound.explanation: The h1-h6 selector applies letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which resolves to -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing reduces character separation and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or low vision (WCAG 1.4.12).
issuesFound.fix: Line 237: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on hero heading
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-content h1 selector applies letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) which resolves to -0.05em. This negative letter-spacing compresses characters and reduces readability for users with cognitive or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 637: .hero-content h1 { letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg) with transition on transform. While there is a prefers-reduced-motion override at line 686, it only sets transform: none and does not disable the transition property itself, which may still cause motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 686: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card has transform: translateY(-2px) on hover with transition on transform. The prefers-reduced-motion override at line 759 only addresses the hover state transform but does not disable the transition property on the base selector.
issuesFound.fix: Line 759: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card has transform: translateY(-3px) on hover with transition on transform. The prefers-reduced-motion override at line 869 only addresses the hover state but does not disable the transition property on the base selector.
issuesFound.fix: Line 869: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card has transform: translateY(-2px) on hover with transition on transform. The prefers-reduced-motion override at line 1015 only addresses the hover state but does not disable the transition property on the base selector.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1015: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item has transform: translateY(-2px) on hover with transition on transform. The prefers-reduced-motion override at line 935 only addresses the hover state but does not disable the transition property on the base selector.
issuesFound.fix: Line 935: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; } .stat-item:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button::after pseudo-element has transform: rotate(90deg) with transition on transform. The prefers-reduced-motion override at line 1165 only sets transition: none on the pseudo-element but the base transform animation may still occur on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1163: .faq-item dt button::after { transform: none; transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator uses box-shadow which is invisible in high-contrast mode
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus selector uses box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2) as the focus indicator after removing outline. Box-shadow is suppressed entirely in Windows High Contrast Mode, leaving keyboard users without a visible focus indicator in that mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 577: #search-input:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query provides no protection
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block exists but contains no CSS rules. This gives a false impression of reduced-motion support while providing no actual overrides for the many transform animations throughout the stylesheet.
issuesFound.fix: Line 453: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph text in section headers uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, failing the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~487: `.section-header p { color: var(--colour-neutral-600); ... }` (using #475569 which provides 5.6:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset property. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users with no indication of focus. This affects all interactive elements that rely on the default focus-visible style.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~345: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: Primary buttons use white text (#ffffff) on a brand primary background (#5588bb), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.7:1. With a font size of 12px (1rem based on html font-size: 12px) and font-weight 600 (semibold), this qualifies as normal text (not large text) and requires 4.5:1 contrast.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~552: Change --colour-brand-primary to a darker shade such as #3a6ea5 (providing ~4.5:1 contrast) or increase font-size to 14px+ with bold weight to qualify as large text.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~312: `body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible rule removes the default outline without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~540: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility on .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This fails the intended purpose of the utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~376: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons include transform in their transition property but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists to disable this motion, which can cause issues for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~519: (add to existing @media block at ~365 or create new) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~610: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This class sets an outline color but does not set an outline width or style, leaving the outline invisible due to the global outline: none rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~363: `.focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links set min-height: 28px, below the recommended 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~648: `[id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button removes the outline on focus-visible with no alternative visual indicator, making it impossible for keyboard users to track focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~596: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible and #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links and buttons remove the focus outline without replacement, leaving keyboard users without focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~622: `#nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus outline without providing a visible replacement, making it invisible to keyboard users when focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~703: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove the focus outline with no visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from seeing which link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~657: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline with no alternative visual indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~726: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class only adjusts outline-offset but does not restore the outline property removed by the global :focus-visible rule. The outline remains invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~359: `.focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons set min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~622: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes are sized at 20px × 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1312: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons set min-height: 32px with vertical padding of approximately 7.5px (var(--space-2-5)), resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~519: `.btn { min-height: 44px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn-lg
issuesFound.explanation: Large buttons set min-height: 36px, below the recommended 44px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~616: `.btn-lg { min-height: 44px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, below the recommended 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~589: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation items set min-height: 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~614: `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets height: 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~677: `#search-input { height: 44px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px, below the recommended 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~699: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties for hover effects, but the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line ~831 is empty and does not disable this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~831: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor element selector removes text underlines with text-decoration: none and provides no alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight change) in the same rule, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for colour-blind users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~324: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all elements, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 463: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline but does not provide a replacement (box-shadow or border) in the same rule-block. Keyboard users cannot identify focused buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 484: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. Since the outline width is zero, the offset renders nothing, hiding focus from keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible replacement. Users navigating dropdown menus via keyboard will lose track of their position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 669: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a replacement. The search button becomes invisible to keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 716: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account nav links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a replacement. Account navigation links are unreachable for keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 733: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. Even with padding, the total height may not reach the recommended 44px touch target size, making it difficult for touch users to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 593: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px. This is below the 24px absolute minimum and far below the 44px recommended size for touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 609: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button

issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes the outline entirely without providing a visible alternative. Users in Windows High Contrast Mode lose focus visibility entirely, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible state suppresses the default outline and provides only an outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating buttons.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which keeps the element in the visual document flow and visible to sighted users, while also failing to hide it from screen readers as intended. This completely breaks the utility's purpose.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]

issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons have a minimum size of 20px × 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target requirement and potentially causing accidental activations or misses.

issuesFound.explanation: Primary buttons use #5588bb background with white text, yielding a 3.5:1 contrast ratio that falls below the 4.5:1 threshold required for normal text (16px semibold), making text difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query overrides :focus-visible to remove the outline entirely (outline: none), leaving no focus indicator in Windows High Contrast Mode. This blocks keyboard navigation for users relying on forced colors.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible modifier only changes outline-offset but inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule. No visible focus ring is rendered for elements using this utility class.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px with no padding, meeting the minimum but falling short of the 44px recommended size without compensatory spacing.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector sets outline-color but does not restore outline-width from the inherited outline: none, resulting in no visible focus indicator for branded focus rings.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button base class includes transform in its transition property but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets both min-width and min-height to 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The active state uses transform: translateY(1px) for a press-down effect without a reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses transform: translateY(-200%) in its default state and transitions this property, but the existing prefers-reduced-motion block is empty and does not disable the transform transition.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline without providing a visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input field sets outline: none with zero offset, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline without providing a visible alternative, making keyboard navigation invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline without replacement, leaving no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus state sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for button elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the outline without replacement, making focused items invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search button has explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, placing it in the 24–43px range without sufficient padding to meet the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The custom checkbox sets width and height to 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to select.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition and uses transform: translateY(-1px) on hover without a reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform transitions and 3D transforms on hover without an effective reduced-motion override (the existing block is empty).

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, violating WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing (AA).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 147: 
  ```
    word-spacing: normal;
  ```

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary button uses white text (#ffffff) on a blue background (#5588bb), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.92:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text under WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) (AA).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: Section header paragraphs use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.14:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on blockquote
issuesFound.explanation: Blockquote text uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.14:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.14:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .product-tagline
issuesFound.explanation: Product taglines use var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on white, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.92:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .price-period
issuesFound.explanation: Price period text uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.14:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .plan-tagline
issuesFound.explanation: Plan taglines use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.14:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .testimonial-card figcaption p
issuesFound.explanation: Testimonial author details use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.14:1 against the white card background.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: Footer copyright text uses var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on the dark footer background (#0f172a), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.4:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove underlines via the global text-decoration: none rule and provide only a color change on hover (#64748b to #5588bb), failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color (A) as color alone distinguishes links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove underlines and rely solely on color changes for hover states, failing to provide a non-color visual differentiator required by WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow. Keyboard users relying on focus indicators will not see where focus is located.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Users navigating forms or actions by keyboard will lose track of the active button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot identify the focused navigation toggle button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the outline on :focus-visible without a visible alternative (box-shadow is only on :focus). Keyboard users may not see the focus ring in the search field.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify the focused search button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot track focus in the account menu.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets a min-height of 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 602: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 20px, which is below the minimum 24px threshold. Small buttons are difficult to tap accurately on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 669: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets width and height of 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons require larger touch areas for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 679: min-width: 44px;
  Line 680: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button has a min-height and min-width of 28px, below the recommended 44px. Mobile users may find the toggle hard to press.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 694: min-height: 44px;
  Line 695: min-width: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links have a min-height of 28px, below the recommended 44px. This degrades the experience for touch users navigating the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 736: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: [id$="-submenu"] li a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links have a min-height of 28px, below the recommended 44px. Touch targets should be larger for easier activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 756: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has a height of 28px, below the recommended 44px. Small input fields are difficult to tap on mobile devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 789: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28x28px, below the recommended 44px. Users may miss the button when trying to submit a search.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 821: width: 44px;
  Line 822: height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible and not hidden. This fails to hide content visually while keeping it for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transform: perspective... rotate... for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) rule for this selector is empty. Motion is not suppressed for users who request it.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card--featured
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover uses transform: translateY... scale..., but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion) rule only targets .pricing-card:hover, not the featured variant. Motion persists for reduced-motion users.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which condenses text and reduces readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 138: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: blockquote foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of 2.85:1. This fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .section-header p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Section header paragraphs use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, failing the 4.5:1 contrast requirement. Low vision users may not read this text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .required-note foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: The required note text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, failing contrast requirements. Important form instructions may be illegible.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .feature-card p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card body text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, failing the 4.5:1 contrast ratio. Text is difficult to read for many users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .step-content p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Step content paragraphs use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, failing contrast requirements. Instructions in the steps may be hard to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .product-body > p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Product card descriptions use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, failing the 4.5:1 contrast ratio. Product details may be illegible.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .plan-tagline foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Plan taglines use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, failing contrast requirements. Pricing information context may be lost.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .price-period foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Price period text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, failing the 4.5:1 contrast ratio. Billing frequency info may be hard to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .filter-label foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Filter labels use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, failing contrast requirements. Users may not understand filter options.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .pricing-toggle > span foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle labels use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, failing the 4.5:1 contrast ratio. Billing period options may be illegible.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .testimonial-card figcaption p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Testimonial metadata uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, failing contrast requirements. Author details may be hard to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .input-hint foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white, failing the 4.5:1 contrast ratio. Helper text for forms may be illegible.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing another non-colour differentiator (like font-weight or border) for the default state. Users with colour blindness may not identify links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as a border or outline. Users in Windows High Contrast Mode will not see focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 279-282: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and provides only outline-offset: 3px without an actual outline width, style, or color. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users without any focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 268-271: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 548: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no compensating padding in the same rule to reach the recommended 44px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1136: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible replacement (box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline). Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 477-479: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content. Content remains visible on screen while being intended for screen readers only.
issuesFound.fix: Line 296-306: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a touch target of only 28px. While this meets the minimum 24px requirement, it falls below the recommended 44px for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 534-537: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 559: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The color var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on background var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555) produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.7:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1136: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-200); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.fix: Line 1129: .footer-brand > p { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-200); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address
issuesFound.explanation: The address element uses color var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on #555555 background (1.8:1), failing the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1130: .footer-brand address { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); line-height: var(--line-height-loose); font-style: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address links
issuesFound.explanation: Address links use color var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on #555555 background (2.7:1), failing contrast requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1132: .footer-brand address a { color: var(--colour-neutral-200); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); display: inline; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The footer bottom paragraph uses color var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) on #555555 background (1.8:1), failing contrast requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1151: .footer-bottom > p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom links use color var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on #555555 background (2.7:1), failing contrast requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1151: .footer-bottom > p a { color: var(--colour-neutral-200); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on global a selector
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight change) in the same or paired rule. Links rely solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 232: a { text-decoration: underline; } OR add a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; } and ensure links have sufficient color contrast.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. outline-offset does not create a visible ring if the outline width is 0. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements that do not override this rule with a visible alternative, blocking keyboard navigation access.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline value. This makes button focus invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see focus on the mobile navigation toggle button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see focus on filter toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see focus on pricing toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Content intended for screen readers only will be visible to all users, cluttering the interface.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p selector uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.3:1, which fails the WCAG AA minimum of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector sets min-height: 32px, which is below the recommended 44px minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes buttons difficult to activate on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum and 44px recommended size. Icon buttons are particularly hard to hit at this size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition properties, causing spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform for .btn.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector uses transition: transform, causing spatial movement. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 858 is empty and does not disable the transform.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text significantly harder to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. While some components add underlines on hover, the default state relies on colour alone to distinguish links from text, failing WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible state removes the outline and only sets outline-offset, leaving no visible focus ring for keyboard users to identify which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from layout
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Content intended only for screen readers will be visible to all users, disrupting the visual layout and potentially exposing private information intended only for assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. In forced-colors/high-contrast mode, this removes the focus indicator entirely with no visible replacement, leaving Windows High Contrast Mode users unable to see focus.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input field. Assistive technologies cannot identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1122: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function toggles the mobile navigation drawer visibility by adding CSS classes and removing the hidden attribute from the overlay, but it never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the menu button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the mobile navigation drawer and updates ARIA attributes, but unlike the open() function, it does not announce the state change via the live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1658: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. This prevents assistive technologies from knowing the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1748: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the _openInstance function after setting inst.open = true.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function animates the panel height and removes the hidden attribute, but it does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". This leaves screen reader users unaware that the answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2448: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the function updates the DOM to show the panel but does not announce the change via a live region. Users relying on screen readers may not be aware that new content is available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1750: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown menu opened');` after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown menu, the function removes CSS classes and hides the panel after a timeout, but it does not announce the closure via the live region. Screen reader users are not notified when the menu disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1770: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown menu closed');` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When closing an FAQ item, the function animates the panel closed and updates ARIA attributes, but it does not announce the collapsed state via the live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the answer is now hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2486: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{title} collapsed').replace('{title}', truncate(item.question, 60))); }` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onAccept missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the user accepts cookies, the function stores the preference and hides the banner, but it does not announce the action via the live region. Screen reader users receive no confirmation that their choice was registered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3383: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieAccepted || 'Cookie preferences accepted');` before calling _hideBanner().

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onDecline missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the user declines non-essential cookies, the function stores the preference and hides the banner, but it does not announce the action via the live region. Screen reader users receive no confirmation that their choice was registered.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3391: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookieDeclined || 'Cookie preferences declined');` before calling _hideBanner().

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function declares a focus trap variable (_trap) and references it in the close() function, but it never initializes or activates the focus trap when opening the mobile menu. Keyboard users can tab outside the open menu into the background content, breaking the modal dialog pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1628: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` after _lockScroll() and before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and locks scroll, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed when the menu opens.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class to the panel, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", leaving the trigger state inaccurate for assistive technology when the dropdown opens.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel and removes the hidden attribute, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", resulting in incorrect state reporting to screen readers when an answer is expanded.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function does not update aria-pressed on filter buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function toggles the CSS.active class on filter tab buttons based on the active category, but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. Screen reader users relying on state attributes will not know which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput._onApply function does not update aria-invalid on input
issuesFound.explanation: The _onApply function validates the discount code and displays an error message in _statusEl if invalid, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the _inputEl. Screen readers will not be notified that the input field itself is in an error state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds an error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies cannot convey the invalid state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2. The markValid() function correctly removes the attribute, but the invalid state is never established.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~892: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function before addDescribedBy.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetric implementation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1942: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` in the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu lacks live-region announcement for open/close actions
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown opens and closes without announcing state changes to screen readers. While focus management is handled, users navigating with screen readers receive no feedback when a dropdown menu appears or disappears, creating a degraded experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2108 (after opening): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`. Line ~2128 (after closing): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", so the state is never communicated to assistive technologies when opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2105: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` in the _openInstance() function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() omits aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The function toggles visual active states on filter tab buttons but does not update aria-pressed (or aria-selected) to reflect the active state. The HTML contract documented in comments expects aria-pressed, yet the JavaScript only manipulates CSS classes, leaving screen reader users unaware which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2844: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing Enter/Space keyboard handling for triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The keyboard handler manages Arrow keys, Home, and End for navigation but omits handling Enter and Space keys. When the FAQ trigger is not a native button (the code adds role="button" and tabindex="0" to non-button elements), users cannot activate the accordion with keyboard, blocking access to the content.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3450: Add cases for `case KEYS.ENTER:` and `case KEYS.SPACE:` that call `e.preventDefault(); toggle(item);` where item is the current trigger's associated FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function is documented to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields, but the code only calls addDescribedBy. This prevents screen readers from announcing the field is invalid, affecting users relying on AT to identify form errors in newsletter, contact, and wizard forms.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds classes and announces state but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to false, creating a state mismatch where AT users are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes hidden and adds classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The close function sets it to false, causing screen readers to miss the expanded state change.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel and announces text but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to false, leaving AT users unaware the answer is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function changes visible content but does not write to a live region. Screen reader users are not notified when a dropdown menu opens, unlike the mobile nav and FAQ components which do announce.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function hides the panel but does not write to a live region. Screen reader users are not notified when a dropdown menu closes, creating a gap in status awareness.

issuesFound.title: Form validation not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the code adds error classes and aria-describedby references to error messages, but it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input fields. Screen readers do not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280 (in markInvalid): After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. Line 4435 (in _renderResult): Ensure `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` is called when `result.valid` is false.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button trigger that controls the mobile navigation drawer does not have an aria-controls attribute linking it to the drawer element. Users cannot programmatically determine which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3565 (in init()): After `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);`, add `if (_drawer && _trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || 'mobile-nav-drawer');` and ensure the drawer element has an id.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu opens, the visual change is not announced to screen reader users via a live region, and focus is not programmatically moved to the menu. Users may be unaware that the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3525 (in open()): After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` and add focus management to move focus to the first item in the drawer or the drawer itself.

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters, the product grid updates and the result count changes, but these dynamic content changes are not announced via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the filter has been applied or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4080 (in applyFilters): After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price values update visually (and are animated), but the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users may not realize the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4320 (in setPeriod): After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (or appropriate message for the selected period).

issuesFound.title: Active filter chips changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are added or removed (indicating active filters), the changes to the filter state are not announced via a live region. Users may not be aware that a filter has been removed or added.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4180 (in render): After updating the chip container, add `if (chips.length > 0) liveRegion.announce('Active filters: ' + chips.map(c => c.label).join(', '));`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation passes or is cleared, the code removes error styling and aria-describedby references, but it does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". Screen readers may still report the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2295 (in markValid): After removing aria-describedby, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`. Line 4450 (in clearField): Add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Plan comparison table headers not keyboard accessible
issuesFound.explanation: The plan comparison table headers (th elements with data-plan-head) are clickable via mouse to scroll to the corresponding plan card, but they are not focusable via keyboard and do not respond to Enter or Space keys. Keyboard users cannot activate these headers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4560 (in init): After `delegate(_tableEl, '[data-plan-head]', 'mouseenter', ...);`, add code to make headers focusable: `qsa('[data-plan-head]', _tableEl).forEach(th => { setAttr(th, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(th, 'tabindex', '0'); th.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); scrollToPlan(th.getAttribute('data-plan-head')); } }); });`.
